title: malaxis ovata
intro: Malaxis ovata, also known as the yellow adder's mouth, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, treating fever, and supporting immune system function. In herbal preparations, the root of the Malaxis ovata plant is typically dried and ground into a powder, then used in teas, infusions, or capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
